No Poverty.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course syllabus supports contributions to eradicating poverty.

Zero Hunger.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course syllabus supports contributions to ending hunger or improving nutrition.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: The course 'Seminar in Psychotherapy' directly and fundamentally promotes mental health and well-being, aligning perfectly with Target 3.4. Both models converged on this high score.

Quality Education.reason: As an MA program, the course provides quality tertiary education and professional skills in psychotherapy, aligning with Targets 4.3 and 4.4. Model A revised to match Model B's accurate assessment.

Gender Equality.reason: The core competency 'cultural awareness and empathy to respect others' (C) implies non-discriminatory practice, indirectly supporting Target 5.1. Model A revised to align with Model B's plausible inference.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: Psychotherapy can indirectly contribute to reducing violence (Target 16.1) by addressing individual psychological issues. Model A revised to align with Model B's plausible, albeit weak, inference.
